description: Recto: Legal query sent to Maimonides along with his autograph responsum. Dating: ca. 1168–1204 CE, based on the years when Maimonides was active in Egypt. This was published by Goitein as well as Blau, Maimonides Responsa, no. 283. Verso: Draft of a long dirge in Hebrew, in the hand of Moshe b. Levi ha-Levi, upon the death of three prominent members of the Jewish community: the rabbis Shemuel, Shelomo and Yiṣḥaq. These three figures were identified by M. A. Friedman as the judges in Maimonides’ court, see Friedman, India Book IVA, pp. 36–37. Shemuel is Shemuel b. Seʿadya ha-Levi, who died in 1203. (Information in part from Amir Ashur's Fragments of the Month, April 2011, November 2016.)
tags: Moshe b. Levi, responsa, autograph, Responsum, Maimonides
description_he: פנים: שאלה הלכתית למיימוני עם תשובתו בכתב ידו, כ-1168–1204. גב: טיוטת קינה עברית בכתב משה בן לוי הלוי על מות שלושה נכבדים: שמואל, שלמה ויצחק — שופטי בית הדין של מיימוני.
